004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.bson;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.math.*;
021import java.nio.charset.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
024
025/**
026 * Specialized output stream for serializing BSON (Binary JSON) streams.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * BSON uses little-endian byte order for all multi-byte values. Documents are length-prefixed: the total
030 * byte count (int32) must appear before the content. Since the size is unknown until all elements are written,
031 * each document is buffered in a {@link ByteArrayOutputStream} child. When complete, the size is calculated
032 * and <c>[int32 size][content][0x00]</c> is written to the parent stream.
033 *
034 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
035 *    <li class='note'>
036 *       This class is not intended for external use.
037 * </ul>
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
040 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Bson">BSON Basics</a>
041 * </ul>
042 */
043@SuppressWarnings({
044   "resource" // OutputStream resource managed by calling code
045})
046public class BsonOutputStream extends OutputStream {
047
048   private static final Charset UTF8 = StandardCharsets.UTF_8;
049   private static final byte BINARY_SUBTYPE_GENERIC = 0x00;
050   private static final byte DOCUMENT_TERMINATOR = 0x00;
051
052   private final OutputStream os;
053   private ByteArrayOutputStream buffer;
054   private boolean inDocument;
055
056   /**
057    * Constructor.
058    *
059    * @param os The output stream being wrapped.
060    */
061   public BsonOutputStream(OutputStream os) {
062      this.os = os;
063   }
064
065   @Override /* Overridden from OutputStream */
066   public void write(int b) {
067      try {
068         target().write(b);
069      } catch (IOException e) {
070         throw new SerializeException(e);
071      }
072   }
073
074   @Override /* Overridden from OutputStream */
075   public void write(byte[] b) {
076      if (b != null && b.length > 0)
077         write(b, 0, b.length);
078   }
079
080   @Override /* Overridden from OutputStream */
081   public void write(byte[] b, int off, int len) {
082      try {
083         target().write(b, off, len);
084      } catch (IOException e) {
085         throw new SerializeException(e);
086      }
087   }
088
089   private OutputStream target() {
090      return buffer != null ? buffer : os;
091   }
092
093   /**
094    * Begin buffering a new document.
095    */
096   public void startDocument() {
097      buffer = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
098      inDocument = true;
099   }
100
101   /**
102    * Finalize the current document and return the bytes (int32 size + content + 0x00).
103    *
104    * @return The complete document bytes.
105    */
106   public byte[] endDocument() {
107      if (buffer == null)
108         return new byte[0];
109      buffer.write(DOCUMENT_TERMINATOR);
110      var content = buffer.toByteArray();
111      // size = 4 (int32) + content length
112      var size = 4 + content.length;
113      var result = new byte[size];
114      writeLE4To(result, 0, size);
115      System.arraycopy(content, 0, result, 4, content.length);
116      buffer = null;
117      inDocument = false;
118      return result;
119   }
120
121   /**
122    * Finalize the current document and write it to the parent stream.
123    *
124    * @param parent The parent output stream.
125    */
126   public void writeDocumentTo(OutputStream parent) {
127      var bytes = endDocument();
128      try {
129         parent.write(bytes);
130      } catch (IOException e) {
131         throw new SerializeException(e);
132      }
133   }
134
135   /**
136    * Writes a BSON element header (type byte + cstring name).
137    *
138    * @param type The BSON type code.
139    * @param name The element name (cstring).
140    */
141   public void writeElement(int type, String name) {
142      write(type & 0xFF);
143      writeCString(name);
144   }
145
146   /**
147    * Writes a 4-byte little-endian integer.
148    *
149    * @param value The 32-bit value.
150    */
151   public void writeLE4(int value) {
152      write(value & 0xFF);
153      write((value >> 8) & 0xFF);
154      write((value >> 16) & 0xFF);
155      write((value >> 24) & 0xFF);
156   }
157
158   /**
159    * Writes a 4-byte little-endian integer to a byte array.
160    */
161   static void writeLE4To(byte[] buf, int offset, int value) {
162      buf[offset] = (byte)(value & 0xFF);
163      buf[offset + 1] = (byte)((value >> 8) & 0xFF);
164      buf[offset + 2] = (byte)((value >> 16) & 0xFF);
165      buf[offset + 3] = (byte)((value >> 24) & 0xFF);
166   }
167
168   /**
169    * Writes an 8-byte little-endian long.
170    *
171    * @param value The 64-bit value.
172    */
173   public void writeLE8(long value) {
174      write((int)(value & 0xFF));
175      write((int)((value >> 8) & 0xFF));
176      write((int)((value >> 16) & 0xFF));
177      write((int)((value >> 24) & 0xFF));
178      write((int)((value >> 32) & 0xFF));
179      write((int)((value >> 40) & 0xFF));
180      write((int)((value >> 48) & 0xFF));
181      write((int)((value >> 56) & 0xFF));
182   }
183
184   /**
185    * Writes an IEEE 754 double (8 bytes, little-endian).
186    *
187    * @param value The double value.
188    */
189   public void writeDouble(double value) {
190      writeLE8(Double.doubleToLongBits(value));
191   }
192
193   /**
194    * Writes a 32-bit integer (4 bytes, little-endian).
195    *
196    * @param value The integer value.
197    */
198   public void writeInt32(int value) {
199      writeLE4(value);
200   }
201
202   /**
203    * Writes a 64-bit integer (8 bytes, little-endian).
204    *
205    * @param value The long value.
206    */
207   public void writeInt64(long value) {
208      writeLE8(value);
209   }
210
211   /**
212    * Writes a boolean (1 byte: 0x00=false, 0x01=true).
213    *
214    * @param value The boolean value.
215    */
216   public void writeBoolean(boolean value) {
217      write(value ? 0x01 : 0x00);
218   }
219
220   /**
221    * Writes BSON null (no value bytes).
222    */
223   public void writeNull() {
224      // Type byte only, no value
225   }
226
227   /**
228    * Writes a BSON string (int32 length + UTF-8 bytes + 0x00).
229    *
230    * @param value The string to write (treated as empty if <jk>null</jk>).
231    */
232   public void writeString(String value) {
233      if (value == null)
234         value = "";
235      var bytes = value.getBytes(UTF8);
236      // BSON string length includes trailing null
237      writeLE4(bytes.length + 1);
238      write(bytes);
239      write(DOCUMENT_TERMINATOR);
240   }
241
242   /**
243    * Writes a cstring (UTF-8 bytes + 0x00, no length prefix).
244    *
245    * @param value The string to write (treated as empty if <jk>null</jk>).
246    */
247   public void writeCString(String value) {
248      if (value == null)
249         value = "";
250      for (var i = 0; i < value.length(); i++)
251         if (value.charAt(i) == 0)
252            throw new SerializeException("CString must not contain null bytes");
253      write(value.getBytes(UTF8));
254      write(DOCUMENT_TERMINATOR);
255   }
256
257   /**
258    * Writes BSON binary (int32 length + subtype byte + raw bytes).
259    *
260    * @param data The raw bytes (subtype 0x00 generic).
261    */
262   public void writeBinary(byte[] data) {
263      if (data == null)
264         data = new byte[0];
265      writeLE4(data.length);
266      write(BINARY_SUBTYPE_GENERIC);
267      write(data);
268   }
269
270   /**
271    * Writes BSON datetime (int64, UTC milliseconds since epoch).
272    *
273    * @param millis The UTC milliseconds since epoch.
274    */
275   public void writeDateTime(long millis) {
276      writeLE8(millis);
277   }
278
279   /**
280    * Writes BSON decimal128 (16 bytes, IEEE 754-2008) from BigDecimal.
281    *
282    * @param value The decimal value.
283    */
284   public void writeDecimal128(BigDecimal value) {
285      if (value == null)
286         value = BigDecimal.ZERO;
287      BsonDecimal128 d128;
288      try {
289         d128 = BsonDecimal128.fromBigDecimal(value);
290      } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
291         // BSON Decimal128 (0x13) is bounded to 34 significant digits with exponent in [-6176,6111]; values
292         // outside that range are an unavoidable lossy case, so fail cleanly instead of leaking an unchecked
293         // NumberFormatException.  See the BsonSerializer limitations javadoc.
294         var e2 = new SerializeException("Value out of BSON Decimal128 range (max 34 significant digits, exponent -6176..6111): {0}", value);
295         e2.initCause(e);
296         throw e2;
297      }
298      writeLE8(d128.getLow());
299      writeLE8(d128.getHigh());
300   }
301
302   /**
303    * Creates a child BsonOutputStream for buffering a nested document.
304    *
305    * @return A new child stream (no parent; use {@link #writeChildDocument} to embed).
306    */
307   public BsonOutputStream createChild() {
308      return new BsonOutputStream(null);
309   }
310
311   /**
312    * Writes the child document's content as an embedded document to this stream.
313    *
314    * @param child The child stream (must have been finalized via endDocument).
315    */
316   public void writeChildDocument(BsonOutputStream child) {
317      // Child buffers to its own ByteArrayOutputStream; we need to write [size][content][0x00]
318      // Child.endDocument() returns [size][content] with 0x00 already in content
319      // So we just need to write the child's endDocument() result
320      var bytes = child.endDocument();
321      // bytes already has size prefix and terminator from endDocument
322      try {
323         target().write(bytes);
324      } catch (IOException e) {
325         throw new SerializeException(e);
326      }
327   }
328
329   /**
330    * Returns whether we are currently buffering a document.
331    *
332    * @return <jk>true</jk> if buffering, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
333    */
334   public boolean isInDocument() {
335      return inDocument;
336   }
337}
